VALUES, ATTITUDES and BEHAVIORAL: Socio-motivational orientation toward others is captured by a prosocial motivation self-report scale.
Attitudes toward debt are quantified by a composite score from the Debt Attitude Inventory.
Parent facilitation of financial autonomy is assessed by a parental support behavior checklist.
Time horizon for financial planning is measured through a delay discounting rate parameter.
Perceived financial wellbeing is self-assessed via the CFPB Financial Well-Being Scale.
Attitudes toward positive financial behavior are recorded by the Financial Management Behavior Scale.
Religious practices are documented by frequency of worship attendance and prayer activity.
Consumer behavior patterns are traced by a household purchase diary over two weeks.
The level of expenditure across consumption categories is derived from bank transaction data.
Attitudes toward money are evaluated by the Money Beliefs and Behavior Scale.
Attitudes toward risk-taking are quantified by a domain-specific risk tolerance questionnaire.
Attitudes toward credit use are captured by a credit attitude survey from the SCF.
Compulsive buying tendency is identified by the Faber and O'Guinn Compulsive Buying Scale.
Delay of gratification ability is assessed by a hypothetical intertemporal choice task.
Financial knowledge level is determined by a standardized test of financial literacy concepts.
Risk aversion index is computed from a series of hypothetical lottery choices.
Perceived debt burden is rated by the individual on a visual analog scale.
Credit card attitudes are measured with a scale on convenience versus debt trap perception.
Subjective financial wellbeing is captured by a single-item happiness question about finances.
Loss aversion coefficient is estimated from a behavioral experiment with real monetary stakes.
Debt tolerance level is assessed by the maximum acceptable loan-to-income ratio.
Credit market trust is evaluated by a set of statements on lender fairness.
The propensity to take financial risks is derived from a general risk question in the SOEP.
Perceived financial vulnerability is quantified by an index of financial anxiety.
Risk tolerance for investment is categorized by portfolio allocation preference.
Attitudes toward mortgage debt are surveyed with a Likert scale on home loan comfort.
Financial distress perception is measured by the InCharge Financial Distress Scale.
Credit score awareness is tested by a question on personal credit rating knowledge.
The belief about debt as a tool for wealth is captured by a forced-choice item.
Financial satisfaction with current debt levels is reported on a ten-point scale.
Risk perception of various financial products is ranked by the respondent.
Debt stigma score is computed from a set of social desirability questions.
The comfort with variable-rate loans is indicated by a binary choice experiment.
Financial optimism about future debt repayment is assessed by a probability estimate.
The attitude toward the use of savings to pay off debt is captured by a hypothetical scenario.
Perceived control over debt situation is measured with a locus of control scale for finances.
Monthly discretionary spending amount is tracked by a personal finance app categorization.
Impulse purchase frequency is recorded in a daily diary for fourteen days.
Brand loyalty index is computed from repeat purchase behavior in scanner data.
Shopping frequency at malls or online is self-reported in a consumption survey.
The preference for experiential over material purchases is evaluated by a forced-choice task.
Compulsive spending cycle duration is identified by the Shulman Compulsive Buying Scale.
Expenditure shock absorption capacity is measured by the liquid assets to monthly spending ratio.
Online shopping cart abandonment rate is derived from browser tracking data.
The tendency to spend windfall income immediately is tested with a hypothetical scenario.
Consumption smoothing behavior is observed via spending changes after income disruption.
Price sensitivity coefficient is estimated from a conjoint experiment on product attributes.
Spending pattern across necessities and luxuries is classified by a machine learning model.
Retail therapy engagement after negative affect is measured by a diary study.
The use of credit for everyday purchases is captured by a payment method preference survey.
Hoarding of discounted goods is identified by a compulsive acquisition scale.
The share of income spent on non-essential items is calculated from budget records.
Overdraft frequency on current accounts is extracted from bank transaction logs.
The number of subscriptions and memberships active is counted from billing statements.
Spending on sustainable products is measured by a green consumer behavior scale.
Budgeting app adoption and usage frequency is recorded by the device analytics.
The ratio of planned to unplanned purchases is computed from pre- and post-shop surveys.
Financial literacy score is obtained from the Big Three questions on compound interest, inflation, and diversification.
The intention to take a loan for education is surveyed by a multiple-choice intention question.
Retirement planning horizon is measured by the number of years until planned retirement.
Knowledge of mortgage terms is tested with a true/false quiz on APR and amortization.
The intent to borrow for a home purchase is captured by a binary variable from a household survey.
Time consistency in intertemporal choices is assessed by a series of staggered rewards.
Financial numeracy level is evaluated by a set of numeracy scale questions.
The willingness to use buy-now-pay-later services is indicated by a hypothetical purchase task.
Future orientation score is derived from the Consideration of Future Consequences scale.
Loan purpose categorization is derived from the free-text description in the application form.
The accuracy of inflation expectations is measured by the deviation from actual CPI.
Financial planning activity index is constructed from a checklist of planning behaviors.
Knowledge of credit card compound interest is tested by a single open-ended question.
The horizon for savings goals is classified as short, medium, or long term by the respondent.
The intent to refinance a loan is captured by a stated preference under different rate scenarios.
Planning for emergency fund adequacy is measured by the months of expenses saved.
Financial confidence in debt management is self-rated on a Likert scale.
The preference for fixed versus variable loan terms is surveyed with a choice experiment.
Long-term care planning awareness is assessed by knowledge of insurance products.
The intention to seek financial advice is measured by a reported probability scale.
Time discount factor for future money is estimated from a series of choice tasks.
The strength of religious belief is measured by the Religious Commitment Inventory score.
Locus of control for financial outcomes is assessed by a Rotter scale adaptation.
The importance of social status consumption is ranked by a material values scale.
Prosocial motivation to donate to charity is captured by a behavioral game with real stakes.
Religious tithing frequency is recorded from church donation records.
The belief in money as a source of security is measured by the Money Attitude Scale.
Spirituality index is computed from a multidimensional scale of spiritual wellbeing.
Altruistic values are assessed by the Self-Report Altruism Scale.
The need for social approval in money matters is measured by a social desirability bias scale.
Ethical consumption beliefs are surveyed with a fair trade purchase intention scale.
Religious salience in daily life is recorded by an experience sampling method.
The value placed on family financial support is assessed by a remittance behavior question.
Materialism level is quantified by the Richins and Dawson Materialism Scale.
The belief in karma or divine financial reward is captured by a custom spirituality item.
Trust in financial institutions is measured by a standard trust question from the World Values Survey.
The motivation to keep up with the Joneses is assessed by a relative income concern index.
The influence of religious prohibition of interest on loan attitude is captured by a Sharia-compliance question.
The desire for financial independence from parents is measured by a young adult survey.
Personal values on work ethic and wealth are derived from the Protestant Ethic Scale.
Social comparison orientation is measured by the Iowa-Netherlands Comparison Orientation Measure.
The importance of leaving a bequest is assessed by a bequest motive question.
Generativity motivation is measured by the Loyola Generativity Scale.
